Career leaderboard — World Championships 2013–2025

Every player pooled across seven World Team Championships (Bali · Chennai · Lyon · Wuhan · Salsomaggiore · Marrakech · Herning), ranked by F4 — trick-taking scored in IMPs vs the same-contract field. Each board is scored against its own championship's field, then board-weighted across the years. Drag the slider to set the minimum board count and watch the ranking settle onto the long-career players.

Per division — a player who appears in Open and Seniors has a separate entry in each (only compared within their own field). Names matched across years; occasional transliteration drift may split a player.

min boards 200 boards  · 
comp bd = boards actually in this analysis — those with a same-contract field (the F2/F3/F4 sample, which shrinks under same-seat) — over total boards declared (the F1 sample; double-dummy needs no field, so every declared board counts and it's evaluated for the actual declaring direction). The left number moves with the seat/side toggle; the right does not. · F1 tricks vs double-dummy (declarer) / holding declarer below DD (defence) · F2 tricks vs the same-contract field · F3 making / defeating vs field · F4 that trick edge in IMPs — the ranking. Green beats the field, red trails it. Champs = number of championships · span. Click any country to highlight it. Seat/side toggle = whether peers must be the exact declarer seat (default) or either seat of the side.

Career F4 regresses toward zero vs single-event figures (more boards, more averaging), so the spread is tighter — a sustained +0.5 IMP/board over 400+ boards across multiple world championships is elite. Declarer, defender-individual and partnership are three separate rankings; defenders are pooled across all partners, partnerships only over boards those two played together.
